qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Reflected XSS via unsanitized jsonp_callback parameter in ajaxedit handler
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes a reflected XSS vulnerability via the jsonp_callback parameter in the ajaxedit handler, which differs from the advisory's stored XSS vulnerability in the BazaR form title field. While both are XSS vulnerabilities, they are different XSS types (reflected vs stored), different components (ajaxedit handler vs BazaR form), different attack vectors, and different persistence mechanisms.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Reflected XSS via unsanitized siteurl parameter in wp-hashcash-js.php
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes a reflected XSS vulnerability via the siteurl parameter in wp-hashcash-js.php, which differs from the advisory's stored XSS vulnerability in the BazaR form title field. While both are XSS vulnerabilities, they are different XSS types (reflected vs stored), different components (wp-hashcash-js.php vs BazaR form), different attack vectors, and different persistence mechanisms.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: None of the two qualifying findings match the advisory-described stored/blind XSS vulnerability in YesWiki's BazaR form title field. Both findings are reflected XSS vulnerabilities in different components (ajaxedit handler and wp-hashcash-js.php). The advisory specifically describes a stored XSS vulnerability where an unauthenticated attacker can inject JavaScript via a form title field that persists in the database and executes when users visit /?BazaR&vue=consulter. Both findings are fundamentally different vulnerabilities with different XSS types (reflected vs stored), different root causes, attack vectors, and components.
evidence: Advisory describes stored XSS in BazaR form title field at /?BazaR&vue=consulter
Finding 'reflected-xss-jsonp-callback-ajaxedit' is reflected XSS in ajaxedit handler via jsonp_callback - different XSS type and component
Finding 'reflected-xss-siteurl-wp-hashcash-js' is reflected XSS in wp-hashcash-js.php via siteurl - different XSS type and component
No finding mentions BazaR, form title field, or the stored XSS attack path described in the advisory
